page: Microsoft Azure E64-16s_v5 is an ESv5-family virtual machine configured with 16 dedicated vCPUs and 512.0 GB of system memory. Powered by an Intel Xeon 8370C processor running at 2.8 GHz on the x86_64 architecture, this instance features 8 physical cores with hyper-threading enabled. The processor includes 48 KB L1d cache, 1 MB L2 cache, and 48 MB L3 cache, alongside hardware instruction support for AVX-512, cryptography acceleration, and virtualization.

Benchmark data places this instance in the average performance tier across several categories. The CPU achieved a stress-ng single-core score of 1898.58 and a multi-core score of 15528.57, while PassMark recorded a CPU score of 21418.74. Memory performance is characterized by a read-only small-block cached bandwidth of 838.79 GB/sec, dropping to 96.24 GB/sec for large-block uncached reads, and a write bandwidth of 55.35 GB/sec. For database and in-memory operations, the instance processed 15.75 million Redis SET operations per second and scored 8079.39 in PassMark database operations.

A primary hardware tradeoff of the E64-16s_v5 is the complete absence of local storage, requiring reliance on external Premium Storage options. Additionally, it lacks integrated GPU hardware, limiting its utility for heavy parallel computing or large-scale AI training, though it manages small-scale LLM inference, processing 1131.28 tokens/sec for a 135M model prompt. From a qualitative cost-efficiency perspective, the high memory-to-vCPU ratio of 32 GB per vCPU offers a dense memory footprint relative to compute resources, making it a specialized option for memory-bound workloads rather than compute-heavy tasks.

This server configuration is designed for memory-intensive workloads, including large in-memory databases, caching layers, enterprise applications, and data processing tasks that require substantial RAM without requiring a proportional scale-up in CPU cores.
description: Microsoft Azure E64-16s_v5 is an ESv5-family virtual machine featuring 16 dedicated vCPUs and 512.0 GB of RAM, yielding a high memory-to-vCPU ratio of 32 GB per core. Powered by an Intel Xeon 8370C processor at 2.8 GHz, it utilizes 8 physical cores with hyper-threading on the x86_64 architecture. The hardware lacks local storage and GPU accelerators, relying entirely on remote Premium Storage. In benchmark evaluations, the instance demonstrates solid memory and database capabilities, achieving 15.75 million Redis SET operations per second and a PassMark database score of 8079.39. Its CPU performance is marked by a stress-ng multi-core score of 15528.57. This configuration is optimized for memory-bound applications, large-scale caching, and database workloads that demand significant memory capacity relative to compute power.
og_description: Microsoft Azure E64-16s_v5 is an ESv5-family virtual machine with 16 dedicated Intel Xeon vCPUs and 512 GB of RAM, optimized for memory-intensive database and caching workloads.

tagline: A memory-optimized virtual machine featuring an exceptionally high RAM-to-vCPU ratio for demanding database and in-memory caching workloads.
bullet_points: 16 dedicated vCPUs powered by an Intel Xeon 8370C processor at 2.8 GHz
512.0 GB of system memory providing 32.0 GB of RAM per vCPU
No local storage included, requiring external Premium Storage
Delivers 15.75 million Redis SET operations per second in benchmark testing
Suited for large in-memory databases, caching layers, and memory-bound enterprise applications
